headline: Relationship pending-actions curation aborts on non-mapping tool_args
hypothesis: The pending-actions curation job crashed while building its insight message because it coerced pending_actions.tool_args with dict(...) before the row-level error guard.

blurb_segments.text: The scheduler reported a failed dispatch for the relationship pending-actions-curation task.

blurb_segments: The job implementation built its operator message from JSONB tool_args before entering the per-row try block.

blurb_segments.text: String-returned or non-object JSONB tool_args values can raise during that conversion and escape the job.

c1.note: The patrol fingerprint identifies the scheduled task and call site that failed.

c2.note: The code path coerced tool_args outside the existing row-level exception handling; the regression test exercises the same row shapes.

evidence_lines.msg: Failed to dispatch scheduled task: pending-actions-curation
Code inspection found pending_actions_curation calling dict(row["tool_args"]) before the per-row try/except.
Regression test covers JSONB tool_args returned as a string and scalar JSONB values without raising.

counter_evidence.hypothesis: The deterministic job registry was missing the relationship pending-actions handler.
counter_evidence.reason: The schedule job_name resolves to a registered relationship handler.

counter_evidence.hypothesis: The relationship roster job module did not expose run_pending_actions_curation.
counter_evidence.reason: The roster module contains the expected run_pending_actions_curation function.

why_this_fix: Normalizing tool_args for display removes the brittle mapping-only assumption, and moving message construction inside the existing row-level guard prevents a single malformed row from aborting scheduled dispatch.
